label: Policy-gradient & actor–critic objectives

node.label: Policy-gradient & actor–critic objectives
node.definition: Policies are optimized directly, often with a learned value or critic to reduce variance and guide improvement.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Policy-gradient & actor–critic objectives is modeled as depending on Reinforcement learning, Bellman, temporal-difference & value objectives and feeding downstream nodes such as Reward-model training, RLHF & RLAIF, Vision–language–action models, Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO).
examples: atlas downstream link to Reward-model training, RLHF & RLAIF

atlas downstream link to Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO)
limitations: A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Reinforcement learning, Bellman, temporal-difference & value objectives.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Policy-gradient & actor–critic objectives has a `uses` dependency on Reinforcement learning because Reinforcement learning is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

In this atlas graph, Policy-gradient & actor–critic objectives has an `optimizes_for` dependency on Bellman, temporal-difference & value objectives because Bellman, temporal-difference & value objectives is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.
In this atlas graph, Policy-gradient & actor–critic objectives has a `uses` dependency on Optimization theory because Optimization theory is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

sentence_units.text: Policies are optimized directly, often with a learned value or critic to reduce variance and guide improvement.

In this atlas graph, Policy-gradient & actor–critic objectives is modeled as depending on Reinforcement learning, Bellman, temporal-difference & value objectives and feeding downstream nodes such as Reward-model training, RLHF & RLAIF, Vision–language–action models, Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO).
atlas downstream link to Reward-model training, RLHF & RLAIF
atlas downstream link to Vision–language–action models
atlas downstream link to Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO)

A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Reinforcement learning, Bellman, temporal-difference & value objectives.
In this atlas graph, Policy-gradient & actor–critic objectives has a `uses` dependency on Reinforcement learning because Reinforcement learning is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

In this atlas graph, Policy-gradient & actor–critic objectives has an `optimizes_for` dependency on Bellman, temporal-difference & value objectives because Bellman, temporal-difference & value objectives is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.
In this atlas graph, Policy-gradient & actor–critic objectives has a `uses` dependency on Optimization theory because Optimization theory is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
